title: Swarm-class battle droid

The '''''Swarm''-class battle droid''' was the [[Ssi-ruu]]k equivalent to a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Ssi-ruuviDroidFighter_egvv.jpg|thumb|left|180px|''Swarm''-class battle droid [[:Category:Technical drawing images|schematics]].]]

''Swarm''-class droids were about two meters long, making them some of the smallest known starfighters. Because of their size and low production costs, Ssi-ruuk vessels could carry hundreds of them into battle—at least 1,500 were present at the first [[Ssi-ruuvi]] [[Invasion of Bakura|invasion]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].

They were tetrahedral and armed with four [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], one at each vertex. These laser cannons could be turned through 360 degrees, giving the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] a wide field of fire, including the ability to fire backwards when chased.

Powered by [[entechment]]—which utilized the drained life sources of sentient beings usually [[P'w'eck]]s and occasionally even [[Human/Legends|Humans]]—each droid used two life-forces. One operated the [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]] while the other piloted the fighter and operated the laser cannons and [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]]. The life-forces in the [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] received their commands from the Ssi-ruuk command vessels via sensor arrays located on thrusters on each side of the vehicle.

Though they had fairly fragile hulls, the droid utilized [[microfilament grid]]s to absorb and redirect incoming energy in its main generator,<ref name="EGVV">''[[The Essential Guide to Vehicles and Vessels]]''</ref> similar to the [[Vangaard Industries|Vangaard]] [[Pathfinder-class scout ship|Pathfinder]]. The fusion thrust technology they employed was somewhat less advanced than equivalent [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] systems and was more easily detected on sensors than many other fighters.

==History==
[[File:Swarm_battle_droid.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A ''Swarm''-class battle droid.]]

The biological origin of the life-forces employed in the power and control systems meant that the droids could be considered a type of [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] rather than a true battle droid. Their strange marriage of technology with biology was believed to have inspired the later [[Shadow Droid]]s designed by [[Umak Leth]] and the reborn [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].

During the Invasion of Bakura in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the fighter type was first encountered by both [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] forces. In large numbers against inferior numbers of foes, the fighters proved devastating. However, they were easily destroyed individually as Imperial, Alliance and [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] pilots would discover.

''Swarm''-class battle droids remained in use as late as the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]]. By that time, the entechment process had been enhanced to even sustain the life forces and the basic technological systems had been improved with reverse-engineered technology from other [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] civilizations, but the droids were still operated by captured life-forces.

Swarms of P'w'eck-piloted battle droids were used during a [[Battle of Bakura (28 ABY)|second attempted invasion]] of [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]]. During the battle, the fighters were equipped with [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors which were used to capture Alliance and Bakuran pilots. However, the battle droids later revolted against their Ssi-ruuvi masters and joined forces with the Alliance and Bakurans following the death of the [[Keeramak]] at the hands of [[P'w'eck Emancipation Movement]] leader [[Lwothin]].
